prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arr, ye be askin' why the heavens above be colored like the finest sapphire in a nobleman's treasure chest? Well, hoist yer ears, for Cap'n will tell ye the God's honest truth of it!

Ye see, the great blue canopy that hangs o'er our heads while we sail the seven seas be blue on account o' the very air itself! The mighty sun, that fiery orb what guides our voyages, sends its rays a-crashin' through the sky like cannonballs through the hull of a Spanish galleon!

But mark me words, these light rays be made of many colors, like a chest o' jewels. When they strikes the wee bits o' air up in the heavens, the blue light gets scattered about like gold coins in a tavern brawl, while the rest sails straight through!

So when ye gaze upward from the deck o' yer ship, 'tis the blue light what reaches yer weather-beaten eyes from all directions across the firmament. That be why the boundless ocean above matches the one below, as if Poseidon himself painted it with his bluest dyes!

Any seafarin' man worth his salt knows this to be true, or may the kraken take me remaining leg! Now pass the rum, for all this learnin' has left me throat as dry as a marooned man's canteen!
